subject: Product Management
title: Dual-Track Agile
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Dual-Track Agile.

hook.text: Dual-track agile splits the product team into two parallel streams: one discovering, one delivering.

buildup.text: The discovery track tests ideas cheaply — prototypes, interviews, experiments. The delivery track builds proven ideas.

discovery.text: Ideas flow from discovery to delivery only when they pass validation. Unproven ideas never reach the backlog.

twist.text: It sounds simple, but most teams resist it. Discovery feels slow when executives are demanding features now.

climax.text: Teams that adopt dual-track ship fewer features but achieve better outcomes — less waste, more impact.

punchline.text: Two tracks, one team. Discover and deliver in parallel.

quiz.question: What is dual-track agile?
options: Running discovery and delivery in parallel, with validated ideas flowing to development
Two separate teams that never communicate
A faster way to skip testing
